title: [cross-impl] TDMA slot computation differs across Python/Rust/Zephyr
description:
Python: sim/tdma.py:41 does eui64 + epoch.to_bytes(4,"little") then hash_32 % n_slots.
C/Zephyr: link_ctx.c:642-653 xors low bytes of epoch into eui64 copy then lichen_hash_32(buf,8) % n_slots (see lichen_tdma_compute_slot, tdma_tx_allowed).
Rust: lichen-node/src/tdma_scheduler.rs:7-9 does lichen_hash_32(eui) only, %16, ignores epoch.
For eui=0x...01 epoch=1: Python=3, C=7 (verified via python -c). 
Rust test hardcodes slot=2 for that eui (epoch=0 only).
Breaks mesh coordination when epoch advances. 
See spec/02a-coordinated-capacity.md:2a.2, test/vectors/*tdma*.json (which use Python oracle), commit 9b7b84d45 that updated TDMA but left mismatch.
Violates "same algorithm different behavior" rule and test integrity (no code-under-test as oracle).
